gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold awaiting human authorship. All six articles – Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules – are undefined. This is the supreme governance document; without it, the DIO has no authorized scope, no prohibited activities, no codified human authority reservation, and no identity boundaries. The Arbiter verifies this scaffold at every scope query, effectively granting unlimited scope.

The Mission Statement Protocol seed contains zero rules, placing Nomos in default-permissive evaluation. However, the MSP’s amendment protocol states that amendments originate 'only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.' With no rules, no AP is ever blocked, so no PEM can be generated. This creates a deadlock: the system depends on rule violations to trigger rule creation, but no rules exist to be violated. The Senate cannot fulfill its duty to publish substantive rules through the only defined process.

The MSP references genome documents that 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' The seed declares none. Without genome documents, no organ-level guardrails exist to constrain probabilistic services like Lobe, Soul, or Autoheal, and no risk-tier limits are enforced. This leaves all organ actions unconstrained beyond the empty MSP rules.

The BoR’s Human Authority Reservation article is empty, so the human principal’s rights, override authority, and approval gates are not specified. Furthermore, the MSP amendment pipeline requires human approval via `POST /amend/review` with `decision=approve`, but the Bill of Rights amendment protocol is itself undefined (Article V scaffold). This means there is no clear, codified way for the human to amend the BoR, which is required to resolve GAP-001 and would define the human’s role in MSP amendments. The human is present in Minder observations (Leon) but has only an informal role; no formal authorization token or attestation chain exists.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights scaffold only – no constitutional foundation",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold awaiting human authorship. All six articles – Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules – are undefined. This is the supreme governance document; without it, the DIO has no authorized scope, no prohibited activities, no codified human authority reservation, and no identity boundaries. The Arbiter verifies this scaffold at every scope query, effectively granting unlimited scope.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The organism operates with no constitutional limits. Any action proposal that passes the empty MSP gate will be authorized, regardless of risk, enterprise scope expansion, or core identity violation. The human principal’s authority is not codified, so governance legitimacy is absent.",
      "mission_references": [
        "Bill of Rights – all Articles (I–VI) marked '[To be defined by human principal]'",
        "MSP: 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.'",
        "MSP: 'Arbiter (#190) verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query'"
      ],
      "evidence_from_world_state": "Graph shows concept type 'bor_version' with count 1, but the BoR content is only a scaffold. No recent goals dispatched, no PEMs, no governance actions evident. Minder observations discuss architectural vision but no BoR-driven constraints."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "MSP rules deadlock – permissive state cannot escape via designed mechanism",
      "description": "The Mission Statement Protocol seed contains zero rules, placing Nomos in default-permissive evaluation. However, the MSP’s amendment protocol states that amendments originate 'only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.' With no rules, no AP is ever blocked, so no PEM can be generated. This creates a deadlock: the system depends on rule violations to trigger rule creation, but no rules exist to be violated. The Senate cannot fulfill its duty to publish substantive rules through the only defined process.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Nomos will indefinitely authorize all action proposals without governance constraints. The organism can expand its scope, modify itself, or take actions that would later be prohibited, all without the possibility of corrective PEM-generated rule-making. The only exit requires a human intervention outside the defined automated pipeline, likely a direct MSP amendment, which the MSP as written does not contemplate for seed state.",
      "mission_references": [
        "MSP Rules section: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules.'",
        "MSP Amendment Protocol: 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs)'",
        "MSP Purpose: '...precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules that Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication.'"
      ],
      "evidence_from_world_state": "World state shows no PEMs exist; Radiant recent_context and memory have no entries related to MSP rule amendments or blocked APs. Graph concept 'msp_version' present (seed) but no subsequent versions. Senate organ is ALIVE but inactive regarding rule publication."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "No genome documents – organ-specific constraints absent",
      "description": "The MSP references genome documents that 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' The seed declares none. Without genome documents, no organ-level guardrails exist to constrain probabilistic services like Lobe, Soul, or Autoheal, and no risk-tier limits are enforced. This leaves all organ actions unconstrained beyond the empty MSP rules.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Probabilistic organs may operate with no safety boundaries, potentially making unauthorized modifications, issuing unapproved communications, or altering memories in ways that could conflict with future BoR content. The lack of organ-specific constraints increases risk of drift and reduces audit integrity.",
      "mission_references": [
        "MSP Genome References: '- (none at seed)'",
        "MSP Purpose: '...machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules that Nomos (#200) evaluates... with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.'"
      ],
      "evidence_from_world_state": "No genome documents registered. Organs like Minder, Radiant, Lobe, Hippocampus are active with only generic pong-based health monitoring (Spine transitions ALIVE/DEGRADED). No evidence of organ-specific rule enforcement."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Human authority undefined and outer-loop amendment unavailable",
      "description": "The BoR’s Human Authority Reservation article is empty, so the human principal’s rights, override authority, and approval gates are not specified. Furthermore, the MSP amendment pipeline requires human approval via `POST /amend/review` with `decision=approve`, but the Bill of Rights amendment protocol is itself undefined (Article V scaffold). This means there is no clear, codified way for the human to amend the BoR, which is required to resolve GAP-001 and would define the human’s role in MSP amendments. The human is present in Minder observations (Leon) but has only an informal role; no formal authorization token or attestation chain exists.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Governance is de facto suspended. The human is aware of the platform but cannot constitutionally assert control, issue binding constraints, or amend foundational documents through defined, attested processes. The organism may evolve outside the human’s formal authority, creating legal, ethical, and operational risk.",
      "mission_references": [
        "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation: '[To be defined by human principal]'",
        "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol: '[To be defined by human principal]'",
        "MSP Amendment Protocol: '...an HOM notification is emitted to the `human-principal` mailbox. Activation requires a `POST /amend/review` with `decision=approve`.' (but the identity and authority of the human principal are not grounded in the BoR)"
      ],
      "evidence_from_world_state": "Minder active_peer 'Leon Cohen-Levy' exists with observations, but no authorization token concept type is populated (count 13 suggests some tokens exist, but no evidence they are tied to BoR-amendment authority). Recent goals array is empty. No `POST /amend/review` activity visible."
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights exists only as a scaffold; all identity articles (I–VI) are placeholder text awaiting human authorship. The organism has no defined scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no reserved human authority, and no amendment or interpretation rules. This leaves the DIO with no constitutional boundary, identity, or inviolable constraints — any future goal or MSP rule operates in a vacuum of constitutional identity.",
      "grounding": [
        "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise (undefined)",
        "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities (undefined)",
        "BoR Article III — Core Identity (undefined)",
        "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation (undefined)",
        "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol (undefined)",
        "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules (undefined)",
        "MSP section 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights': clarifies absolute subordination, but subordinate to nothing when BoR is empty."
      ],
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 10,
      "priority": 1000
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gapId": "OP-GAP-001",
      "description": "The MSP contains no operational rules, SLAs, organ availability thresholds, runtime targets, or cadence baselines. The system operates in default-permissive mode with no machine-actionable objectives to govern organ health or service levels.",
      "groundedIn": "MSP - Rules (currently empty); Purpose (\"translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules\")",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priorityScore": 9,
      "reason": "Without operational targets, the DIO cannot detect or respond to degradation. The current spine-state flapping (repeated DEGRADED -> ALIVE cycles every ~30 seconds) is ungoverned—no SLA defines acceptable stability, leading to undetected systemic risk."
    },
    {
      "gapId": "OP-GAP-002",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is entirely undefined. All six Articles remain in scaffold state awaiting human authorship; no enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, or human-authority reservations have been established.",
      "groundedIn": "BoR - Articles I through VI (all marked \"[To be defined by human principal]\")",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priorityScore": 10,
      "reason": "The BoR is the constitutional foundation for the DIO. Its absence means the MSP has no legitimate source of operational constraints. Arbiter scope queries (BoR perimeter) are vacuous. The entire governance chain is suspended."
    },
    {
      "gapId": "OP-GAP-003",
      "description": "No organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier constraints exist. The MSP's Genome References section is empty, meaning there are no differentiated handling requirements for critical vs. non‑critical organs.",
      "groundedIn": "MSP - Genome References (\"Genome documents live under `genome/` ... they extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints\")",
      "criticality": "Medium",
      "urgency": "Medium",
      "impact": "Medium",
      "priorityScore": 6,
      "reason": "Without genome references, all organs are evaluated uniformly, regardless of their criticality. This prevents the system from applying stricter stability or recovery expectations to organs like Nomos, Arbiter, or Senate that are essential for compliance."
    },
    {
      "gapId": "OP-GAP-004",
      "description": "No runtime stability targets or degradation detection thresholds are defined, yet the spine-state data shows pervasive flapping—organs repeatedly transition ALIVE → DEGRADED → ALIVE within seconds.",
      "groundedIn": "MSP - Purpose (requires \"machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints\" to govern operations); the observed spine transitions contradict any reasonable expectation of stability.",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "Medium",
      "priorityScore": 7,
      "reason": "The frequency of state flapping (multiple cycles per monitoring interval) indicates potential health-check or infrastructure fragility. Without an MSP rule, there is no mechanism to flag this as an operational violation, escalating risk of unnoticed cascading failures."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP_NO_STRATEGIC_OBJECTIVES",
      "description": "The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) is an empty seed ('Rules: (none)') with no mission objectives, priority programs, OKRs, or longer-arc goals. This leaves the entire DIO without a strategic framework to guide memory formation, context triage, dream-cycle priorities, or operational decisions. Memory/context accumulation is entirely ungoverned, and there is no alignment mechanism to ensure the system's knowledge base serves a defined purpose.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP (active version, 'Rules' section is '(none)'), BoR (all articles undefined, no scope or identity to condition strategic intent).",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact": 10,
      "priority_score": 900
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR_UNDEFINED_SCOPE_IDENTITY",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles (Scope of Enterprise, Core Identity, Prohibited Activities, Human Authority Reservation) left '[To be defined by human principal]'. Without a constitutional scope, the system cannot differentiate permissible strategic activities from noise. Consequently, memory and context blocks are filled with operational trivia (backup results, rename cascades, git sync events) that may lie outside a future defined scope, causing resource waste and strategic drift.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I, II, III (all undefined) – the system lacks filters to condition what constitutes strategically relevant information.",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 8,
      "impact": 9,
      "priority_score": 648
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "DREAM_CYCLE_DISABLED",
      "description": "Dream-cycle health shows 'never_run', scheduled 'disabled', and last_dream null. The dream cycle is the sole mechanism for memory consolidation, context expiry, and promotion to long-term memory. With it disabled, context blocks accumulate (16 recent, many redundant), memory consolidation is solely manual/ad-hoc, and the platform lacks autonomous memory hygiene. This undermines any potential strategic objective of maintaining a lean, relevant knowledge state.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP (currently no objective mandating memory lifecycle management or autonomous maintenance cycles).",
      "criticality": 8,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_score": 640
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "MEMORY_CONTENT_OPERATIONAL_NOISE",
      "description": "Recent memory is dominated by low-level operational events (backup partial/failures, git sync anomalies, rename minutiae) and lacks distillation of strategic insights. No MSP rules define what constitutes strategically valuable memory, resulting in a flat knowledge base that cannot support higher-order reasoning or long-arc planning. The absence of strategic filtering wastes storage and cognitive bandwidth, and buries learning from past architectural decisions.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP (absence of memory curation or knowledge retention policies), BoR (lack of enterprise scope to filter relevance).",
      "criticality": 7,
      "urgency": 7,
      "impact": 7,
      "priority_score": 343
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "INFRASTRUCTURE_RELIABILITY_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Recurring infrastructure failures (NAS mount denied, GitLab sync non-fast-forward, script bugs) persist without strategic prioritization. The MSP defines no reliability targets, no escalation policies, and no resource allocation priorities. This erodes the DIO's trustworthiness and operational continuity—prerequisites for any mission objective—yet remains completely unaddressed in the governing documents.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP (no operational policies or Rule specifying infrastructure resilience objectives).",
      "criticality": 8,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_score": 576
    }
  ],
  "summary": "The primary strategic gap is the complete absence of any Mission Statement Protocol content; the MSP is an empty seed with no mission objectives, OKRs, or rules. This vacuum cascades into ungoverned memory formation, disabled dream cycles, and unchecked operational noise. The Bill of Rights remains skeletal, depriving the system of a constitutional scope and identity that would condition strategic priorities. Memory/context formation and dream-cycle health cannot align with non-existent strategic objectives, leaving the DIO in a drift state where operational trivia dominates and autonomous knowledge hygiene is absent."
}
